title: Test coverage for internal/conceptmap (CI-bound, untested)

evidence.ref: Audit found internal/conceptmap had no test file despite gating every PR via `conceptmap verify` in contracts.yml. Pattern regressions would only be caught if the existing concept-map.riido.json file happened to trigger them; new code paths or edge cases were untested
039-secretscan-tests - same priority-order rationale (CI-bound + critical → test first); D040 extends to conceptmap
036-bcs-enum - established the `validRecord()` helper idiom (each test flips one field) now mirrored as validMap() in conceptmap_test.go

guards.ref: internal/conceptmap/conceptmap_test.go - 14 tests covering Validate per-field rules (version, PascalCase, kind enum, owner enum, description+examples required, duplicate names, relationship referent-existence, relationship-kind enum, constraint id pattern, duplicate constraint ids, guard_candidate.status enum) + Query semantics (case-insensitive, unknown returns nil, rels mentioning name, constraints mentioning name in rule OR rationale)

examples: TestValidateAcceptsMinimalMap proves the validMap() helper is itself valid (regression catch if a future schema change breaks the helper).
TestValidateRejectsBadVersion covers empty/short/extra-dots; documents that 'v1.2.3' currently PASSES (the check is just dot-count). Tightening is a separate decision.

TestValidateRejectsNonPascalConceptName documents that nameRe accepts all-uppercase 'DECISION' because the project has acronym-heavy names like IRSchema and GraphQLQuery. The test covers what IS rejected: lowercase start, hyphens, digit start, empty.
counterexamples: 4 untested internal/ packages remain (devpattern, irdrift, jsonutil, knowledge). irdrift is the next CI-bound priority; the others are operator-internal.

Tests document current behavior — they do NOT tighten Validate rules. Behavior changes (e.g. stricter version regex, stricter name regex) require their own decision because they could reject existing on-main concept names or version strings.
